rationale: Severe mismatch on every axis: the archetype is a high-intensity anime power-fantasy hook (Goku Super Saiyan, screaming, punch attack) with a saturated yellow/orange/black palette and male shounen-action audience — none of which aligns with Marble Sort's calm pastel-lavender aesthetic, no-character physics-sorting loop, or the 25-55 female casual-puzzle audience. The only thread of overlap is the abstract "satisfaction" emotional pitch, but it's expressed through aggression rather than the tactile/relaxing satisfaction the game delivers. Do not produce; if the team wants to chase the satisfaction emotion, brief a separate ASMR/oddly-satisfying sorting archetype instead.
